narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hypocritical and alarmist towards Russia and China, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Western diplomatic stances and actions.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: This quote highlights and seemingly advocates for a catastrophic nuclear war outcome, blaming 'un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries”' for leading humanity to this point, which predicts and emphasizes severe negative consequences stemming from Western governance.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly questions the reliability and framing of Western media coverage of the Ukraine war, accusing it of prolonging the conflict, which fits the narrative of undermining confidence in media sources.

narratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
narratives.reasoning: This question, posed in the promotional text, directly evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear escalation in the conflict, which is the core of the 'amplifying war-related fears' narrative.
The title and premise of the interview explicitly predict a specific outcome of the war—that Russia is winning. This is a direct speculation on the conflict's result.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically America) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of portraying the West's reactions as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This implies that the West, through its media, is deliberately prolonging the war in Ukraine for its own reasons (e.g., to weaken Russia), disregarding the resulting Ukrainian deaths, which fits the narrative that the West is self-interested and indifferent to Ukraine's fate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: yes but where is what he says getting us …seems nowhere
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement expresses futility about the impact of truthful discourse (like Scott Ritter's), implicitly suggesting that diplomatic or informative efforts are ineffective and lead nowhere, discrediting the potential of such efforts.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West by portraying Western (specifically American) populations as morally bankrupt, uncaring, and deserving of destruction. It is a broad, vitriolic condemnation that fits the parent narrative but does not align with any of the more specific diplomatic subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and overreaction to Russia and China is a persistent, misguided policy. It implies that this aggressive posture, which includes sanctions and confrontation, is based on a false premise ('SELDOM a threat') and is therefore a self-inflicted negative consequence for the West, aligning with the idea that such actions are counterproductive and backfire.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote presents a catastrophic, nihilistic view of the conflict's escalation, directly blaming 'un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries”' for pushing the situation toward nuclear war, which is framed as a final, devastating negative consequence for the West (and the world) resulting from Western political actions and leadership.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' framing it as a tool that withholds or distorts information, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media institutions as instruments of propaganda.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of distrust towards media by accusing Western media of a specific, harmful misrepresentation ('framing the war as if Russia is losing') that has dire consequences. It discredits Western media but does not fit the more specific definitions of being an 'instrument of propaganda' or targeting Ukrainian media, thus it is a remainder supporting the general narrative.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This direct question explicitly raises the possibility of nuclear weapon use in the context of the Ukraine war, evoking fear about such an outcome.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This sarcastic and despairing statement frames the continuation of the current conflict as a direct path to a full-scale nuclear war (WWIII), amplifying fears of catastrophic escalation.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement amplifies war-related fears by blaming unelected Western officials for leading humanity toward catastrophe, supporting the parent narrative of amplifying fear but not fitting a more specific subcategory.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title directly states 'Russia is Winning,' which implies the opposing force, Ukraine, is losing. This aligns with the subnarrative definition of suggesting the Ukrainian military is experiencing a significant decline in effectiveness or strength.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement counters a speculated outcome (that Russia is losing) and argues that this false framing prolongs the war. It directly engages in speculation about war outcomes, supporting the parent narrative, but does not fit the specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory.
